title: Ranunculus lawalreei
intro: Ranunculus lawalreei, commonly known as Lawalree's buttercup, is a plant species in the genus Ranunculus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible roots. The health benefits of Ranunculus lawalreei include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ranunculus lawalreei is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Ranunculus lawalreei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This genus is commonly known as the buttercup family. It is a type of alpine buttercup and grows in high-altitude regions.
